elements.name: Title
texts: "Such dismal and loansom Places are very apt to make frightful and melancholy Impessions upon the Minds for Weak and Ignorant"

elements.name: Description
texts: A critique of St. Patrick's Purgatory and Catholic pilgrimage in general.

elements.name: Source
texts: The Great Folly, superstition and idolatry of Pilgrimages in Ireland, especialy of that to St. Patrick's Purgatory: together with an account of the loss that the publick sustaineth thereby, truly and impartially represented, p. 2

elements.name: Contributor
texts: Digitised by Collections numérisées - Université de Rennes 2

elements.name: Text
texts: "[Lough Derg] is almost inaccessible by Horsemen, even in Summer, because of great Bogs, Rocks, and Precipices with which it is environed on all Sides. Such dismal and loansom Places are very apt to make frightful and melancholy Impessions upon the Minds for Weak and Ignorant; and, therefore, it is very probably, that it was pitched upon, as a convenient Place for passing so grand an Imposture upon them."
